# Judgment Heuristic

**Definition:** The cognitive process of answering a difficult question by unconsciously substituting it with a simpler, automatically generated [[Basic Assessment]] or via [[Intensity Matching]].

## Systems Dynamics & Relationships
The [[Judgment Heuristic]] is the primary mechanism of the **Cognitive Effort Homeostasis Loop**. When [[System 2]] attempts to process a difficult question, the resulting cognitive load triggers the [[Mental Shotgun]], which rapidly fires off extra computations. The availability of these easy, substitute answers (the heuristic) immediately decreases the operational demand on [[System 2]], balancing cognitive load and returning the brain to an effortless resting state. 
- **Upstream (Driven by):** [[Basic Assessment]], [[Intensity Matching]]
- **Downstream (Drives):** [[System 2]] (decreases operational activation)

## Behavioral Engineering Context (COM-B)
This heuristic acts as a powerful **Motivation** driver. Humans are biologically driven to resolve cognitive conflict and will naturally adopt the most accessible associative answer provided by [[System 1]].
- **Leverage Point (Cognitive Disfluency):** Introduce minor environmental friction (Capability/Opportunity)—such as harder-to-read fonts, mandatory delays, or required procedural steps—to disrupt the cognitive ease that fuels the [[Judgment Heuristic]], thereby artificially catalyzing [[System 2]] engagement.
